title: 3 Opening and Operating a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
body_text:
3	Opening and Operating a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
3.1	Opening a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
The Province of BC or its agent provides operating instructions to your financial institution via a new account letter and notice to Central 1 to activate the sweep. The letter includes the following information:
Authorization for agents of the BC Services office or business to make deposits for settlement to the Province of BC
The address of the Ministry to which the financial institution forwards monthly statements
Confirmation that Central 1 is the only party that is permitted to initiate account withdrawals
Instructions for the collection of account fees, as applicable
Additional details and instructions on operating the account
Open a Provincial Concentrator holding account for the Province of BC as follows:
Request and review the new account letter for the Provincial Concentrator Holding Account. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Provincial Concentrator Holding Account â New Account Letter
Verify the agentâs identity and follow internal procedures for opening accounts. The Province of BC Concentrator must be in Canadian funds. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program, see The Guide (BC), Business Accounts Part, for more information.
File the new account letter in the Provincial Concentrator holding account member file along with the account documentation.
Once the account is opened, the Province of BC notifies Central 1 to activate the sweep.
3.2	Operating a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
Only Central 1 is authorized to debit a Provincial Concentrator holding account. The account must never be in overdraft.
You canât debit misdirected chargebacks (dishonoured cheques) to the account. See Section 5.3, Managing Chargebacks for a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account, for more information.
Account service charges and/or fees must not be charged against the account. See Section 3.4, Reimbursement for Provincial Concentrator Holding Account Service Charges and Other Fees, for information on reimbursement for account charges.
3.3	Provincial Concentrator Holding Account Inquiries
Provincial Concentrator holding accounts are owned by the Province of BC. You must disclose account balances and other account information upon request to authorized individuals from the Province of BC. For security and privacy, contact Central 1 Payment Services Specialists to confirm the names of authorized Province of BC employees before providing account information. See Section 1.2, Contacts and Websites.
3.4	Reimbursement for Provincial Concentrator Holding Account Service Charges and Other Fees
Since Provincial Concentrator holding accounts are for deposit only, you canât debit an account for fees of any kind (e.g., maintenance fees, service fees, overdraft charges due to a processing error).
To obtain reimbursement for account maintenance fees from the Province of British Columbia, invoice the Province of BC via email to
BankingRelations@gov.bc.ca
or write to:
Provincial Treasury
Attn: Banking & Contract Analyst
PO Box 9414 STN PROV GOVT
1st Floor â 620 Superior Street
Victoria BC V8W 9V1
3.5	Changes to a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account Transit and Account Number
To advise Central 1 of changes to a Provincial Concentrator holding account transit or account number:
Submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow. See Section 5.2, Managing Deposit Errors for a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account, for procedures on creating a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow.
Enter the new account and/or transit in the Detail Description field.
You must submit the request at least one week before the effective date. Failure to advise Central 1 can cause processing delays and errors.
END
